observations.recommendation: 42. Direct the SWM Focal Person to implement the programs and projects relative to Solid Waste Management during the year for an efficient utilization of the budget.
43. Direct the SWM Focal Person to implement the programs and projects relative to Solid Waste Management during the year for an efficient utilization of the budget.

1.16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) create an Inventory Committee, who will be temporarily relieved of all
their regular duties, to conduct the complete physical inventory in three (3)
months or less;
b) require the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and
the submission of a copy to the COA Audit Team, at least ten (10) calendar
days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and
c) supervise the conduct of the physical count and the immediate preparation
of a Report on Physical Count of PPE using the prescribed form and the
submission of the report to the Audit Team;
d) Initiate the implementation of all prior years’ audit recommendations on PPE
account, as well as the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts”
as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
e) Consider the appointment of a General Services Officer (GSO) who shall
take charge of property management.

1.17. Further, we reiterate our previous year’s recommendations that the Designated
Property Officer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare and maintain a
Property Card and Property Ledger Card, respectively, for each asset of the LGU
based on the RPCPPE.

2.10. We recommended that the Property Custodian summarize all the Requisition
and Issue Slips (RIS) issued during the year in a Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit to the Municipal Accountant to support
the cost of the inventories recorded as consumed/issued in the books.

2.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant only recognize in the
books the issued/distributed/consumed inventories based on the report of the
Property Custodian.

3.17. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make representation with the
Bank Manager (LBP Naval) that the bank statement be furnished to the LGU
promptly to enable the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank
Reconciliation Statements on time.

3.18. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the payees
of checks which are about to be stale, and issue a replacement check for those stale
checks after having these determined eligible for replacement.

3.19. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
a) prepare the entries to revert the stale checks to the Cash in Bank account and to
set up the payables account;
b) record immediately the unrecorded deposits;
c) determine the cause of the unaccounted discrepancies between the cash in bank
balances per books and per bank; and
d) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record all the identified reconciling
items.

4.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry
voucher to record the donated motorboat to its proper Property, Plant and
Equipment (PPE) account and the corresponding depreciation of the asset
pursuant to IPSAS 17.

5.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) require the Municipal Accountant and the Chairperson of the Bids and
Awards Committee to submit to the Audit Team an explanation on the
deficient/defective Performance Security of the contracts presented in
Table 1;
b) require the Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
require the Contractors to post additional Performance Security with a
validity period that covers another one year from completion date and to
submit a Performance Security with validity period for those security that
were not indicated;
c) henceforth, require the BAC Secretariat to examine the validity period of
the Performance Security posted by the winning bidders before signing the
contract and ensure that the validity period is correctly indicated and have
it confirmed with the Insurance Company; and
d) issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance one year after the completion of
the project.

5.14. The management was amenable with the recommendations.

6.17. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, the MDRRM Officer and other
Officials concerned:
a) submit justifications why disbursements were made using the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on expenditures which
were not in accordance with its intended purposes; and
b) utilize the LDRRMF solely and strictly, for disaster-related activities as
mandated in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 20131.

6.18. We further recommend that the MDRRM Officer:
a) exert best efforts to implement the PPAs in the LDRRMFIP;
b) submit an explanation on the delayed submission of the monthly utilization
reports; and
c) submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within the
reglementary period.

6.19. The management commented that they will abide with the recommendations

7.11. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry to
correct the reported Trust Liability-DRRMF in the Trust Fund books in the
amount of ₱10,024.00.

7.12. We also reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer transfer
the unexpended balances from CY 2016-2020 and CY 2021 amounting to
₱2,063,438.94 and ₱1,296,911.11, respectively to the DRRM depository
account.

8.10. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the balances of the affected accounts.

8.11. We also recommend that the MDRRM Officer furnish the Sangguniang Bayan
a copy of the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF
showing the amount of the unexpended balances from CY 2012-2015 totaling
₱1,992,106.73, which shall be the basis of the latter in its preparation and
issuance of a resolution reverting the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to
the unappropriated surplus.

9.9. We recommend that the Municipal DRRM Officer in coordination with the
LDRRM Council:
a) include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan under separate caption, the list
of PPAs to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF from prior years;
and,
b) review the balances of the Continuing Appropriations in the preparation
of the annual budget to determine which of these projects are no longer
implementable and have this re-appropriated for other disaster-related
projects.

10.10. We recommend that Municipal Mayor as Chairman of the Inventory, Disposal
and Appraisal Committee to initiate the disposal of all unserviceable and
damaged properties of the LGU, following the procedures outlined in COA
Circular No. 89-296 to prevent further deterioration and exposure to possible
theft.

10.11. The management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

11.12. We recommend that Municipal Treasurer as Petty Cash Custodian to strictly
comply COA Circular No. 2012-001 on the documentary requirements for the
replenishment of petty cash fund.

11.13. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant-Designate meticulously
check the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents of all
replenishment of petty cash fund.

1. We reiterate our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor should constitute an Inventory Committee who shall conduct a physical count of all LGU properties, mark counted assets with property tags and prepare and submit the RPCPPE with complete details to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnish the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian; 2. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide the Designated Property Officer with the list of tangible items costing ₱15,000.00 and below appearing as PPE in the books for the issuance of ICS to the persons responsible for the items; and 3. re-classify the said items to the appropriate Inventory account duly supported with duly signed ICS. 4. prepare adjusting entries to correct the misclassification of the procured equipment as stated in paragraph 1.10. 5. maintain complete PPE Ledger Cards, and 6. Finally, we recommend that the Property Custodian shall maintain Property Cards for all properties of the LGU and reconcile these with the Reports on the Physical Count of PPE.

8. We recommended that the concerned personnel in-charge on the distribution of drugs and medicines and laboratory supplies to submit every end of the month the RSMI supported with duly filled-up RIS to the accounting unit which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant to record the issuances during the month.

9. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should transfer the unexpended balances of all unexpended DRRM fund to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books. 10. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an entry to transfer the remaining balances from Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs to Trust Liability-DRRM account and prepare subsidiary ledger by source of donor to conform with COA Circular 2012-002 in accounting and reporting of LDRRMF.

11. We reiterate our previous years audit recommendation that the management should transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE amounting to ₱5,097,348.41 which is not in accordance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, thus, posing the risk of the funds being used for other purposes. Also, the balances of the accounts Due to Other Funds (unexpended DRRMF-GF) and Due from Other Funds (unexpended DRRM-TF) with that of the Trust-Liabilities-DRRMF Account balance should be reconciled. 12. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should trace and locate relevant documents to serve as basis for adjusting entries to put the accounts Due to Other Funds in the GF books, Due From Other Funds and Trust Liabilities-DRRM in the Trust Fund books.

13. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive submit the distribution list duly signed by the actual recipients/beneficiaries and any evidence to support the distribution of food and relief assistance and the essential emergency medicines.

14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit justification why these transactions were indorsed for payment despite the deficiencies of the supporting documents. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Suspension or Disallowance, if warranted.

15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development Council should identify and include in the AIP projects which are ready for implementation in the current budget year to ensure its completion. 16. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare the adjusting entries to capitalize the additional costs in the Repair and Rehabilitation of Multi-purpose Building which were either recorded as expense or as Other Structures.

17. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should direct the Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documentary requirements required under COA Circular 2012-001 including the proof that conditions cited under DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-5 were complied with. 18. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit explanation why the voucher was recommended for payment despite the lacking supporting documentary requirements.

19. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should ensure that Contracts/Purchase Orders entered into by the LGU and Contractors/Suppliers contain the contract duration of the project within five (5) working days from the time of its execution and the expected delivery dates for the procurement of goods, as guide in contract implementation and at the same time protect the interest of the LGU from delayed completion of projects or delivery of goods; 20. We reiterate our recommendation that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and/ or personnel in charge with the preparation of the Contract/Purchase Order should strictly comply with the provision of aforementioned COA Circular No. 2009-001 and submit copy of perfected Contracts, POs and MOAs within five working days upon approval together with the required supporting documents to the Auditor for review and evaluation; and 21. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should refrain from processing claims without complete documentation.

22. Prepare monthly the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), supported with pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) of every office/end-user; also attach Distribution List from Program Coordinators; 23. submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of the following month as basis in recording the issuances as deductions in the Inventory account; and 24. Maintain an updated and complete Stock Cards for every supply and material procured by the LGU, using the same as basis of stock level for succeeding procurement and as guide in monitoring prices. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant: 25. maintain an updated and complete Supplies Ledger Cards; 26. reconcile monthly with the Stock Cards of the Property Officer; and 27. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all inventories of the LGU and submit the RPCI on the prescribed dates.

28. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review past transactions per bank account and compare with bank in order to identify the variances; prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements of all depository bank accounts of the Municipality of Maripipi; record required reconciling items with journal entries in the GL, where necessary, to ensure the correct balance of the Cash in Bank accounts.

29. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remit the full amount of taxes withheld within ten (10) days after the end of each month to avoid penalties and surcharges.

30. Constitute an Inventory Committee who shall conduct a physical count of LGU properties and prepare the RPCPPE with complete information; and 31. Constitute a Disposal Committee who shall undertake the disposal of unserviceable properties of the LGU.
32. Determine the purposes of the available cash in the bank and provide such information to the Local Development Council; 33. Implement the said programs and projects immediately.

34. Obtain the MOA and Work and Financial Plans from the Source NGA for every fund received; 35. Furnish the Audit Team with the said documents within five days of receipt thereof; 36. Implement all programs within the time frame prescribed in the MOA to avoid idle funds; 37. Coordinate with the designated Municipal Engineer in the implementation of projects to ensure completion within the time frame prescribed in the MOA; 38. Return to the source agencies the unexpended funds once the programs or projects is completed or cannot be undertaken by the LGU for valid reasons.
39. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF in the General Fund and Special Trust Fund, and; 40. Report not only local DRRMF but also calamity fund coming from other government agencies.

41. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit to the designated Municipal Accountant, within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, all financial documents and accounts and reports for immediate recording of the transactions in the books of accounts.

1.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the
Inventory Committee to:
1.16.1 complete the physical count of all LGU properties;
1.16.2 prepare the required RPCPPE on or before December 31, 2020 in the
prescribed format with all the required information such as the
model, serial number, condition, property number, acquisition cost or
assessed value; and
1.16.3 reconcile or tally RPCPPE with records of the Property Officer and
Municipal Accountant.
1.17 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1.17.1 record all procurement in the Subsidiary Ledgers, reconcile SL
balances with the General Ledgers and provide one Property Ledger
Card for each PPE account; and
1.17.2 convene with members of the Road Network Inventory Committee,
Property Officer and the Municipal Engineer to resolve the
composition of the accounting records and facilitate the preparation of
the Road Networks Inventory Report.
1.18 Further, we reiterate our previous year’s recommendations that the
Designated Property Officer:
1.18.1 issue Property Acknowledgement Receipt for every equipment issued
to an employee to track the custodians of LGU assets. Renew PAR
every after three years to account for any losses.
1.18.2 provide a Property Card for each asset of the LGU based on the
RPCPPE and every time there are newly procured assets; and
1.18.3 mark assets with Property Tags counted during Physical Inventory.

2.9 We recommended that the designated Property Officer:
2.9.1 Prepare monthly the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI), supported with pre-numbered Requisition and Issue Slip
(RIS) signed by end-users and the SSMI of every office/end-user; also
attach Distribution List from Program Coordinators;
2.9.2 submit to the Municipal Accountant every 5th day of the following
month as basis in recording the issuances as deductions in the
Inventory account; and
2.9.3 maintain an updated and complete Stock Cards for every supplies and
materials procured by the LGU, using the same as basis of stock level
for succeeding procurement and as guide in monitoring prices.
2.10 We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant:
2.10.1 maintain an updated and complete Supplies Ledger Cards;
2.10.2 reconcile monthly with the Stock Cards of the Property Officer; and
2.10.3 reconcile accounting records with the RPCI and prepare valid
adjustments in case of variances.
2.11 Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory
Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all inventories of the
LGU and submit the RPCI on the prescribed dates.

3.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review past transactions
per bank account and compare with bank statements in order to identify the
variances; prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements of all
depository bank accounts of the Municipality of Maripipi; record any valid
reconciling items in the GL, where necessary, to ensure the correct balance of
the Cash in Bank accounts.

4.11 We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the Local Development Council,
coordinate with the Punong Barangays and consult the Provincial Government of
Biliran, in the identification of projects to be implemented by the LGU and include
only in the Annual Investment Plan those that are attainable during the year.
4.12 We also recommend that the Municipal Engineer:
4.12.1 submit to the Secretariat of the Bids and Awards Committee the Program of
Works and Detailed Estimates of the projects listed in the Annual
Investment Plan (AIP) to set the schedule of the public bidding;
4.12.2 complete the implementation of all projects that had been bidded in order to
maximize the utilization of the 20% DF; and
4.12.3 report to the Mayor any unimplemented project for evaluation by the LDC
in the review of subsequent year’s budget.
4.13 Lastly, we also recommend that the Chairman and Members of the Bids and Awards
Committee conduct the public bidding on the projects identified in the Annual
Investment Plan without delay.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the Municipal
Treasurer, open and maintain a separate bank account as Special Trust
Fund for all unexpended LDRRMF which are transferred from the General
Fund.
5.10 We also recommended that the Municipal DRRMO implement fully the
activities in the LDRRMFIP to fully equip the LGU in the management of
risk and effect of disasters.

6.8 We recommended that the Members of the Oversight Committee designated
by the Municipal Mayor:
6.8.1 review the current fees and charges imposed by the LGU;
6.8.2 compute the appropriate rates for each services rendered by the
LGU, and;
6.8.3 submit to the Municipal Mayor the proposed revisions of the
rates and charges.
6.9 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairperson of the
Oversight Committee, recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan the immediate
approval of the proposed revisions of the LGU’s Municipal Revenue Code.
6.10 Further, we recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor, as the Presiding
Officer of the Sangguniang Bayan, facilitate the immediate issuance of a local
revenue ordinance for the adoption of the revised rates on fees and charges of
the LGU.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer remit the full amount of taxes withheld within ten (10) days after
the end of each month to avoid penalties and surcharges.

8.6 We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO:
8.6.1 Verify and ensure that the net take home pay of all employees of the
Municipality of Maripipi is not below P5,000.00, the minimum
amount prescribed by law;
8.6.2 Verify the paying capacity of an employee before signing loan
applications so that the mandated net take home pay of the employee
will be complied;
8.6.3 Authorize deductions only if the loan was obtained from accredited
financing institutions enumerated by law, and exclude private lending
institutions.

9.6. We recommended that the GAD Focal Point implement the programs and
activities included in the DILG-approved GAD Plan and Budget of the LGU
and integrate in the GAD Accomplishment Report other regular activities of
the LGU which can be considered GAD-related.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council, through the Mayor as the Chairman, include only in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU projects which are capital or investment in nature so that the 20% Development Fund will be utilized for socio-economic and environmental development. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer accelerate the implementation of projects contained in the Annual Investment Plan so that the same are fully implemented during the year and the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the 20% Development Fund only for projects and programs authorized to be paid therefrom.
observations.reason: Various programs and projects were not implemented during the year and there are still some administrat ive and operating expenses incorporate in the Annual Investment Plain for CY 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the SWM Focal Person to implement the programs, projects and activities relative to Solid Waste Management during the year for an efficient utilization of the budget for the said program. Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that the LGU and its barangays are fully compliant with the requirements of the Solid Waste Management Act especially in the operation of sanitary landfill and construction of Materials Recovery Facility in order to protect the health of its constituents and preserve the environment.
observations.reason: Non-acquisi tion of lot yet to be used for garbage disposal and or sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point to coordinate with the offices/divisions concerned to ensure that the programs/projects identified in the GAD Plan are fully implemented.
observations.reason: Lack of coordinatio n between the local person and other officials concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant always require the submission of ORs to support claims for taxi fares otherwise, no payments should be made for such purpose. Also, review thoroughly the completeness of the supporting documents and ensure the legality, correctness and reasonableness of the amounts being claimed before payments for travelling expense are approved. We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all employees to exercise prudence when filing claims for official travels and observe strictly the existing provisions of the afore-cited laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Laxity of the LGU officials and employees to secure official receipts for their taxi fares.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC, through its BAC Secretariat submit the required documents as mandated by COA Regulation. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensures the immediate submission of the said documents.
observations.reason: Due to the distance from the Island to the COA office.

observations.recommendation:
1.14  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:


     a) Designate a Property Officer for  the  LGU  to  manage,  secure  and
        provide maintenance on all properties of the Municipality;


     b) Constitute an Inventory Committee who shall conduct a physical count
        of LGU properties and prepare the RPCPPE with complete  information;
        and


     c) Constitute a Disposal Committee who shall undertake the disposal  of
        unserviceable properties of the LGU.


1.15  We also recommended that the designated Property Officer:


     a) Maintain Property Cards for all properties of the Municipality;
     b) Provide Property Tags for each equipment with complete labels, and;
     c) Update PAR of each employee every three years.


1.16 The recommendations of the Audit Team were well taken during  the  exit
    conference.

3.9         We recommended that the Municipal Mayor,  the  Chairman  of  the
    BAC, the BAC members, the TWG, the BAC Secretariat and  the  designated
    Municipal Engineer:

    a. Submit the COMPLETE procurement  documents  enumerated  in  Table  1
       relative to the Design and Build contract with  A.  C.  Rivero  Dev.
       Corp.,  especially  documents  relative  to  the   Variation   Order
       including suspension of work and extension of contract time, if any;


    b. Review strictly and thoroughly the documents of prospective  bidders
       in accordance with the provision of Republic Act No. 9184.

3.10  The Municipal engineer stated during the  exit  conference  that  some
    documents were inadvertently not submitted to COA and commits to submit
    them immediately and would  look  into  other  deficiencies  that  need
    rectifications.   He further stated that no further payments  had  been
    made to the contractor which may result to payment of  variation  order
    due to some defects found during the construction of the project

4.16  We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant  and
    each Program Coordinator or Focal Person involved in the implementation
    of programs and projects funded by the National Government:

    a. Obtain the MOA and Work and Financial Plans from the Source NGA  for
       every fund received;


    b. Furnish the Audit Team with the said documents within five days from
       receipt thereof;


    c. Implement all programs within the time frame prescribed in  the  MOA
       to avoid idle funds;


    d.  Coordinate  with  the  designated   Municipal   Engineer   in   the
       implementation of projects to  ensure  completion  within  the  time
       frame prescribed in the MOA;


    e. Transfer to Trust Fund bank account, within five days from  receipt,
       the LGSF received from General Fund bank account to avoid the use of
       the fund for other purposes;


    f. Return to the source agencies the unexpended funds once the programs
       or projects is completed or cannot be  undertaken  by  the  LGU  for
       valid reasons, and;


    g. Update the subsidiary ledgers on fund transfers.

4.17  Recommendations of the Audit Team were  well  taken  during  the  exit
conference.

13. We recommended that the MDRRMO together with the MDRRM Council:


   1) Prepare the LDRRMFIP using the format provided  in  Annex  A  of  COA
      Circular No. 2012-002 and submit to COA at the start of each year;


   2) Review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include activities  to  be
      funded from the  prior  years’  Special  Trust  Fund.   Any  revision
      thereon must bear the approval of the SB.


    We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


    3) Initiate the transfer at year-end of  the  unutilized  current  year
      LDRRMF to the Special Trust Fund;
    4)      Maintain subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF in the General Fund  and
      Special Trust Fund, and;
   5) Report not only local DRMMF  but  also  calamity   fund  coming  from
      other government agencies


5.14  During the exit conference, the DRRMO stated  that  she  had  prepared
    the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format starting CY  2019
    and had updated the plan then while the Municipal Accountant  may  seek
    the assistance of  the  former  Accountant  in  identifying  unutilized
    LDRRMF.

6.7   We recommended that the Municipal Engineer: (1) submit  to  the  Audit
    Team  the  List  of  all  on-going  government  PPAs  and   those   for
    implementation at the beginning of each  year;  (2)  include  non-infra
    activities in the quarterly reports of publicized PPAs; (3) provide all
    the necessary information in  the  project  signboards  regarding  each
    project being undertaken by the Municipality; and (3) inform the  Audit
    Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project
    or before the  start  of  the  program/activity  that  the  appropriate
    project signboards are already posted.

6.8   The Municipal Engineer had stated that he had submitted  the  list  of
    all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and  those
    that are to be implemented during the year to the Municipal  Accountant
    and further stated that compliance  with  the  recommendation  will  be
    undertaken in 2019.

We recommended that the MDRRMO prepare the LDRRMFIP in the prescribed format, present the 30% QRF in lump-sum and include a separate caption for balances from the previous years in the MDRRM-Trust Fund. Likewise, we recommended that the MDRRMO, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the “Report on Sources and Utilization” on the LDRRMF periodically and submit the same promptly to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.reason: Laxity of the MDRRMO to prepare the prescribed format of LDRRMFIP, hence reiterated this audit observation in this year's audit report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute the Local Council for the Protection of Children in the LGU from among its department heads, with himself as the Chairman. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the created LCPC to prepare the LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for integration in the local development plan and ensure that funds for LCPC-identified programs, projects and activities for child protection and welfare and prevention of juvenile delinquency is equivalent to 1% of the IRA of the Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace, verify and investigate the discrepancies between the balances per books and per inventory report and effect appropriate adjustments to reconcile both records. We also reiterate our prior year’s recommendations requiring Management to conduct the physical inventory of PPE at least once a year and submit a report on the results thereon. We further recommended that the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) on issued properties be renewed every three (3) years.
observations.reason: Laxity of the Inventory Committee and the designated Municipal Accountant to conduct inspection and physical count of all equipment and properties of LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned coordinators to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided in the MOA and require them to submit the liquidation reports and other documents to the source agency immediately.
observations.reason: Some programs and projects are difficult to attain.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% Development Fund are fully utilized for the implementation of projects that will provide environmental, socio-economic and developmental benefits to the residents of the locality.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower Reiterated in Part II

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor give top priority in the procurement of the rescue vehicle as part of its pre-disaster assets to partake in rescue related operations in times of calamities.
observations.reason: Laxity of the concerned officials in pursuing the procurement of rescue vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor request from the Provincial Government of Biliran for a copy of the required quarterly accomplishment report.
observations.reason: The concerned official to ask copy of the quarterly accomplishment report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality should do the following: a. As indicated in Section 13 of the MOA, the municipality should inform the Developer in writing of any violation of the terms of the said MOA, giving him reasonable time, from receipt of the service notice, to correct or rectify the infraction(s) committed. Further, such service notice or written notice of violation should be notarized as required by law. b. If the Developer failed to comply with the said notarized service notice, the municipality should rescind the said MOA as provided in Section 12 and takeover the operation of the eco-tourist beach resort in accordance with Section 15 thereof.
observations.reason: The developer is given due process as regards to his noted violations. Demand letter issued

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant trace, verify and investigate the  discrepancies  between  the balances  per  books  and  per  inventory  report  and  effect  appropriate  adjustments  to  reconcile  both  records.  We also  reiterate  our  prior  year’s  recommendations  requiring Management to conduct the physical inventory of PPE at  least  once a  year and submit a report on the results thereon.  We further recommend  that  the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) on issued properties be  renewed every three (3) years.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor   require   the   concerned
coordinators to fast-track  the  implementation  of  programs  and  projects
funded by the National Government in accordance with the  timeline  provided
for in the MOA and require them to submit the liquidation reports and  other
documents to the source agency immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the 20% Development
Fund are fully  utilized  for  the  implementation  of  projects  that  will
provide environmental, socio-economic  and  developmental  benefits  to  the
residents of the locality.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  give  top  priority  in  the
procurement of the rescue vehicle as part  of  its  pre-disaster  assets  to
partake in rescue related operations in times of calamities.

We recommend that the Municipal  Mayor  request  from  the  Provincial
Government of Biliran for a copy of the  required  quarterly  accomplishment
report.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor initiate and follow-up with  the
NSWMC for the immediate approval of the municipality’s ten (10) year SWMP.

We recommend    
that Management 
should submit   
the said Report  
as required by  
COA regulations.

We recommend    
that the APP-CSE
should be       
submitted       
regularly every 
November 30 of  
each year so as 
to ensure the   
availability of 
the projected   
CSE in the      
DBM-PS SubDepot.

We recommend    
that the BAC,   
through its BAC 
Secretariat,    
ensures that all
documents       
required        
pertaining to   
procurement be  
completely      
attached in the 
claims made by  
Contractors.    
Also, we        
recommend that  
the Municipal   
Accountant      
should be more  
circumspect in  
reviewing the   
supporting      
documents       
attached to a   
claim before    
processing it

We recommend    
that the        
Municipal Mayor, 
being the LCE,  
should act with 
urgency in the  
mandatory       
closure of the  
existing open   
dumpsite as he  
may be held     
liable for      
violating the   
solid waste     
management law.

We recommend    
that the        
Municipal Mayor 
should regularly
appoint a DRRM  
Officer as      
required by law.

We recommend    
that the        
Municipality    
should do the   
following:      
a. As indicated 
in Section 13 of
the MOA, the    
municipality    
should inform   
the Developer in
writing of any  
violation of the
terms of the    
said MOA, giving
him reasonable  
time, from      
receipt of the  
service notice, 
to correct or   
rectify the     
infraction(s)   
committed.      
Further, such   
service notice  
or written      
notice of       
violation should
be notarized as
required by law.
b.If the        
Developer failed
to comply with  
the said        
notarized       
service notice, 
the municipality
should rescind  
the said MOA as 
provided in     
Section 12 and  
takeover the    
operation of the
eco-tourist     
beach resort in
accordance with
Section 15      
thereof.
observations.reason:
The       
developer 
is given  
due      
process as
regards to
his noted 
violations
. Demand  
letter    
issued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend    
that procurement
of regular and  
ordinary        
supplies and    
equipment be    
made in bulk as 
required by COA 
rules and       
regulations.

We recommend    
that the BAC    
should prepare  
the Procurement 
Monitoring      
Report (PMR) and
submit the      
approved PMR to 
the GPPB in the 
prescribed      
format as       
required by law.
Also, we        
recommend that  
the Municipal   
Mayor ensures   
the preparation 
and submission  
of the required 
report.

We recommend    
that the BAC,   
through its BAC 
Secretariat     
submit the      
required        
documents as    
mandated by COA 
Regulation. We  
also recommend  
that the        
Municipal Mayor 
ensures the     
immediate       
submission of   
the said        
documents.
observations.reason:
Due to the
distance from the
island to
the COA
Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to |require the     
concerned       
officials or its
equivalent to   
strictly observe
the provisions  
of Section 361 
of GAAM Volume I
on the          
submission of   
mandatory       
reports,        
relative to the 
use of          
government      
vehicles.

We recommend    
that the        
Municipal Mayor, 
together with   
its GAD Focal   
Point System,   
should oversee  
and coordinate  
with the DILG   
Regional Office 
in order to      
facilitate the  
approval of the 
its Annual GPBs.
Further, we     
recommend that  
the local       
sanggunian      
should first    
obtain the      
approved GPB    
before          
incorporating it
and enacting the
Annual Budget of
the              
municipality.

DILG did
not demand
for the

observations.recommendation:
We recommend    
that the        
Municipal Mayor 
should ensure   
timely          
submission of   
the LGU’s GPB   
and GAD AR to   
the Audit Team  
as required by  
COA regulations.

We recommend    
that the        
Municipal Mayor 
should render a 
careful         
evaluation of   
the request for  
special         
monetization as 
such is required 
by CSC rules and
regulations.
